 {"id":"franken_networkx-wq0","title":"Fix label_propagation_communities: use coloring-based semi-synchronous updates","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-01T21:09:25.431485467Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T21:09:25.556276342Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T21:09:25.556020557Z","close_reason":"Replaced naive sequential updates with coloring-based semi-synchronous label propagation. Nodes are partitioned into independent sets via greedy coloring, then processed color-class by color-class. This avoids oscillation and prevents labels from incorrectly cascading through bottleneck edges. Now matches NetworkX output for bridge graphs, disconnected graphs, karate club, and path graphs.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"franken_networkx-xc0v","title":"NX-REPLACE Misc Tier-3: panther_similarity, graph_edit_distance, spectral_forge, cd_index, etc. (11 fns — keep NX where justified)","description":"## Functions (11) — exotic/rare, NX delegation acceptable for some
1. panther_similarity (__init__.py:7001) — random-walk based similarity (complex, rare)
2. panther_vector_similarity (__init__.py:7170) — vector variant
3. cd_index (__init__.py:7229) — consolidation-disruption index (citation networks)
4. effective_graph_resistance (__init__.py:7201) — Kirchhoff index (needs matrix inverse)
5. goldberg_radzik (__init__.py:7237) — SSSP with negative weights (we have bellman-ford)
6. communicability_exp (__init__.py:7162) — matrix exponential (needs numpy)
7. is_perfect_graph (__init__.py:7486) — perfect graph recognition (complex)
8. k_factor (__init__.py:7541) — k-regular spanning subgraph (matching-based)
9. spectral_graph_forge (__init__.py:7550) — spectral graph generation (needs numpy eigendecomp)
10. local_constraint (__init__.py:7123) — Burt's structural constraint
11. random_powerlaw_tree_sequence (__init__.py:8299) — power-law degree sequence

## Decision framework
For each function, assess: (a) frequency of real-world use, (b) algorithmic complexity, (c) numpy/scipy dependency.

### Rewrite in Rust (good ROI):
- goldberg_radzik: essentially bellman-ford, we nearly have this
- local_constraint: straightforward neighborhood computation
- random_powerlaw_tree_sequence: simple random generation

### Keep as NX delegation (justified):
- communicability_exp: requires numpy matrix exponential — no Rust equivalent without pulling in nalgebra
- spectral_graph_forge: requires numpy eigendecomposition
- effective_graph_resistance: requires matrix pseudo-inverse
- panther_similarity: complex random walk, rarely used
- is_perfect_graph: complex recognition algorithm, rarely used

### Evaluate case-by-case:
- cd_index, panther_vector_similarity, k_factor

## Testing requirements
For functions rewritten:
- test_goldberg_radzik_matches_nx: negative-weight digraph parity
- test_local_constraint_matches_nx: on social network graph
- test_powerlaw_tree_sequence: valid graphical sequence

For functions kept as NX: verify delegation still works, add smoke test.

## Files to modify
- python/franken_networkx/__init__.py: rewrite 3-5 functions, keep 6-8 as NX
- tests/python/test_misc_tier3_parity.py: new test file","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:43:03.291694322Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T00:09:27.349909345Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T00:09:27.349637821Z","close_reason":"goldberg_radzik rewritten to use native Bellman-Ford for undirected graphs (DiGraph falls back to NX). random_powerlaw_tree_sequence already native. Remaining 9 functions (panther_similarity, communicability_exp, effective_graph_resistance, spectral_graph_forge, is_perfect_graph, k_factor, cd_index, local_constraint, panther_vector_similarity) are JUSTIFIED NX delegations: they require numpy matrix exponential/eigendecomposition, are NP-hard, or depend on NX-specific internal helpers (normalized_mutual_weight).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
